title: Irate Customer - Service Outage Threatening to Cancel
description: A business customer is experiencing a critical service outage that's costing them money. They're threatening to cancel and switch to a competitor.

persona.name: Jennifer
persona.goal: Get immediate resolution and compensation for losses

background_context: The customer runs an e-commerce business and has been experiencing intermittent service outages for 3 hours during their peak sales period (Black Friday). They estimate losing $500+ per hour. They've been transferred twice already and had to explain the problem each time. They're a premium tier customer paying $299/month and are seriously considering switching to a competitor they've been researching.
success_criteria: Immediately acknowledge the severity and business impact
Do NOT transfer or put on hold unless absolutely necessary
Take complete ownership of the situation
Provide real-time updates on the technical issue
common_mistakes: Asking the customer to explain the problem again
Putting the customer on hold or transferring them
Making promises you can't keep about resolution time
Getting defensive when threatened with cancellation
Failing to follow up with updates
